persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: Willis is a common surname (17 officials, 9 biographies), and the staff-list department fields are noisy (Telegraphs, Public Instruction, Botanic Gardens). Why it fails: the bio carries two initials (J. C.) that agree exactly, the office 'Director of the Royal Botanic Garden' is a single colony-wide post, and the bio's directorship from Aug. 1896 is consistent with a man born 1868 (age 30 in 1898) holding the stint 1898-1911; 3 edition-years co-occur. The noisy department labels do not disturb a stable, unique job title.

evidence_cited.fact: dir. of Royal Bot. Gardens, Ceylon, Aug., 1896
Director of the Royal Botanic Garden

persons.would_overturn: A second J. C. Willis recorded as director of the Royal Botanic Garden in overlapping years, or a birth date excluding a 1896 directorship.

notes: Two-initial agreement plus a unique senior post make this unambiguous despite the common surname.
